text.title: Deploy Scripts: Output Examples and Scenarios
text.summary: Annotated deploy output examples: single service, full stack, rollback after bad deploy, migration hook, and SDK cascade redeploy.

example.code: $ bash ~/workspace/deploy/deploy.sh nero-core Pre-deploy checks passed [DEPLOY] nero-core Requirements changed, installing... Restarted nero-core.service nero-core: OK [DONE]

$ bash ~/workspace/deploy/deploy.sh all Pre-deploy checks passed [DEPLOY] nero-sdk — library synced (no restart) [DEPLOY] nero-infra — docker compose up -d [DEPLOY] nero-core — synced, deps installed, restarted: OK [DEPLOY] nero-channel-web — synced, deps installed, restarted: OK [DEPLOY] nero-channel-tg — synced, deps installed, restarted: OK [DEPLOY] nero-web — static files synced: OK [DONE]

section.title: Rollback After Bad Deploy
section.p: A code change in nero-core introduced a ModuleNotFoundError. Rolling back to HEAD~1.

example.code:
# Detect failure $ journalctl --user -u nero-core --since "5 min ago" | tail -5 nero-core[12345]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'nero_core.new_feature'
# Roll back $ bash ~/workspace/deploy/rollback.sh nero-core [ROLLBACK] nero-core: abc1234 → HEAD~1 [DEPLOY] nero-core nero-core: OK [ROLLBACK DONE] now at def5678 To return to main: git -C ~/workspace/repos/nero-core checkout main && deploy.sh nero-core
# Verify $ systemctl --user is-active nero-core.service active $ curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/health | jq .status "ok"

section.title: Deploy with Database Migration Hook
section.p: nero-channel-web has a `post-sync.sh` hook that runs Alembic migrations after code sync.

example.code: $ bash ~/workspace/deploy/deploy.sh nero-channel-web Pre-deploy checks passed [DEPLOY] nero-channel-web [hook] post-sync INFO [alembic] Running upgrade abc123 -> def456, add user preferences table Migrations complete Restarted nero-channel-web.service nero-channel-web: OK [DONE]

section.title: SDK Cascade Redeploy
section.p: When nero-sdk gains a new model field, all consuming services need redeployment so they pick up the updated editable install.

example.code:
# nero-sdk was updated $ git -C ~/workspace/repos/nero-sdk log --oneline -1 abc1234 feat: add message priority field
# Deploy SDK first, then all dependents $ bash ~/workspace/deploy/deploy.sh nero-sdk [DEPLOY] nero-sdk — library synced (no restart)
$ for svc in nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg; do bash ~/workspace/deploy/deploy.sh "$svc" done # Each service's pip install -e . now picks up the updated SDK

$ bash ~/workspace/deploy/pre-deploy-check.sh === Pre-Deploy Validation === [PASS] .env exists [PASS] Runtime /srv/nero exists [PASS] Disk: 283000MB free [PASS] systemd user session active [PASS] DATABASE_URL set [PASS] REDIS_URL set [PASS] ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set [PASS] Python3: 3.12.8 [PASS] Redis: responding [WARN] nero-core: uncommitted changes === Results: 0 errors, 1 warning — ready to deploy ===

$ bash ~/workspace/deploy/status.sh === NERO Platform Status === systemd: nero-core ACTIVE nero-channel-web ACTIVE nero-channel-tg ACTIVE nero-web ACTIVE docker: nero-postgres Up 3 days (healthy) nero-redis Up 3 days (healthy) nero-rabbitmq Up 3 days (healthy) health: nero-channel-web ok (port 8100) resources: Memory: 8.2Gi/30Gi Swap: 0B/4.0Gi Disk: 12G/310G (4%) Load: 0.45 0.38 0.32
